name: Python Hash Maps Assembly
description: Assemble dictionary and frequency-counting code patterns.

questions.question: Assemble code to initialize an empty frequency dictionary.
questions.explanation: Use {} or dict() to create a dictionary.

questions.question: Assemble code to increment frequency of key ch with default 0.
questions.explanation: get with default allows safe increment.

questions.question: Assemble code to initialize count for key word to 0 only if missing.
questions.explanation: setdefault inserts default only when key absent.

questions.question: Assemble loop over dictionary key-value pairs in counts.
questions.explanation: items() yields key and value together.

questions.question: Assemble condition to check if key target is present in map m.
questions.explanation: Use in for key membership in dict.

questions.question: Assemble code to fetch key k from dict m with default -1.
questions.explanation: get returns provided fallback when key missing.

questions.question: Assemble code to delete key k from dict m only if it exists.
Assemble code to iterate keys of dict d.
Assemble code to merge d2 into d1 in place.
questions.explanation: Both update() and |= modify dict in place.

questions.question: Assemble code to create inverse map from pairs list.
questions.explanation: Dict comprehension can invert key-value pairs.

questions.question: Assemble code to initialize adjacency list entry for node n if absent.
Assemble code to append neighbor b to graph[a].
questions.explanation: Append neighbor to the adjacency list.

questions.question: Assemble code to count words in text list words using Counter.
Assemble code to check anagram equality by frequency maps a and b.
questions.explanation: Frequency maps equal means same counts.

questions.question: Assemble code to return first index of target from map idx with default -1.
questions.explanation: Use get for safe map lookup.
